title: Lalu Prasad Yadav moves Delhi High Court challenging criminal charges in land-for-jobs case
description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) assured the Delhi High Court on Wednesday that it will not put approvers on witness stand in the land-for-jobs case against Lalu Yadav and his family till the High Court decides the former railway minister's challenge to the framing of criminal charges. Additional Solicitor General (ASG) DP Singh made the statement before Justice Manoj Jain. An approver in a criminal trial is an accomplice or co-accused who has confessed to a crime and has agreed to test
